title: Residential Support Worker
description: We are seeking experienced Youth Residential Support Workers to provide care and safeguarding for young people in mainstream residential settings. This is a temporary, ad-hoc role with flexible shifts, including earlies, lates, sleep-ins, and waking nights. You will support young people in the care system, helping them build independence and resilience while managing complex needs and risks. Key details include Leeds-based work, uplifted rates for wake nights and weekends, and a mix of shift patterns. The role includes person-centred care, emotional support, record-keeping, safeguarding awareness and support with routines, education and daily living. Previous experience in a similar setting is required, along with a strong understanding of safeguarding and ligature risk.
summary: Flexible residential care work supporting young people in Leeds, with varied shifts and weekend or waking-night uplifts.

title: Youth Residential Support Worker
description: This temporary Leeds role supports young people aged 12-17 in mainstream residential homes. The focus is on providing stability, promoting independence and maintaining a safe, nurturing environment for children in care. Work is ad-hoc and flexible, with earlies, lates, sleep-ins and waking nights available. The brief also calls for experience in residential childcare, youth support or safeguarding, plus strong de-escalation and risk management skills. Ligature training is mandatory. This role suits someone who wants flexible hours while still working directly with young people who may display challenging behaviours and need consistent support in day-to-day residential care.
summary: Leeds-based temporary youth support role focused on stability, safety and day-to-day care for children in residential settings.

title: Relief Extra Care Support Worker
description: Creative Support is recruiting a Relief Extra Care Support Worker for its Hampton Crescent service in Leeds. The service includes beds and flats for older adults who require extra care. The role covers practical and emotional support, with an emphasis on wellbeing, personal care, domestic tasks and helping service users access work, education and social opportunities. The employer is looking for a reliable and proactive worker who can demonstrate positive regard and work flexibly across evenings on a roster basis. Previous care experience is required. The description points to a people-facing role focused on helping older adults maintain independence and achieve day-to-day wellbeing goals in a structured extra care environment.
summary: Extra care support role in Leeds helping older adults with wellbeing, daily living and personal care across a 40-bed service.

title: Residential Childcare Support Worker (Sessional/Casual)
description: This Leeds-based sessional childcare role offers ad-hoc cover in a residential setting. LCS is looking for committed and enthusiastic support workers who can cover shifts on a casual basis as needed. The brief points to waking-night residential childcare work, with flexible hours rather than a fixed schedule. It is a straightforward care role for someone who wants occasional residential support shifts rather than a permanent rota. The main appeal is flexibility within a children's residential care environment.
summary: Casual Leeds childcare support work covering ad-hoc shifts, suited to residential care staff wanting flexible hours.

title: Residential Childcare Support Worker
description: LCS is recruiting a full-time Residential Childcare Support Worker in Leeds. The role is set at 35 hours per week plus sleep-in shifts, with the opportunity to take on additional hours. The advert describes a structured residential childcare post suited to someone committed to regular frontline support work with children and young people. Salary information in the source also references additional earning potential through sleep-ins. This is a stronger fixed-hours option than the sessional post, aimed at someone seeking a more stable residential childcare role in West Yorkshire.
summary: Full-time Leeds residential childcare role with regular sleep-ins and scope for extra hours in a structured care setting.

title: Assistant Residential Support Workers
description: This Halifax role supports adults with mental health challenges, learning disabilities and other complex needs in a residential setting. The position is rota-based and focused on helping residents live as independently and safely as possible. It is described as a non-personal-care role, with emphasis on relationship-building, daily living support, monitoring wellbeing and working with wider professionals. The source also mentions record-keeping, medication support and financial support tasks. Experience in adult residential care is preferred, especially around mental health or learning disabilities. This is a practical residential support post for someone comfortable working within a rolling rota and supporting adults with complex needs.
summary: Halifax residential support post helping adults with complex needs live more independently on a rota-based schedule.

title: Complex Care Assistant
description: This Brighouse complex care role is aimed at a female applicant supporting a client with specific care needs. The source states that the worker must be a driver with access to a car and confirms that sponsorship is not available. It also notes that a permanent contract may be available after successful probation. The role appears to be focused on direct one-to-one complex care support in the community, with the gender requirement set out in the source under the Equality Act 2010. This is a targeted care assistant post for someone able to travel and deliver specialist support.
summary: Brighouse complex care role for a driver, supporting a female client with specialist day-to-day care needs.

title: Complex Care Assistant
description: This Huddersfield complex care role supports a client with specialist care needs and requires the worker to be a driver with access to a car. The source states that sponsorship is not available and notes that a permanent contract may follow a successful probation period. The advert points to direct complex care support in a one-to-one setting, with delivery requirements similar to the Brighouse role. Salary fields have been left blank because the source salary metadata was unusable for annual formatting, even though the body text mentions an hourly figure.
summary: Huddersfield complex care role for a driver, supporting specialist care delivery on a contract basis.

title: Behaviour Support Worker
description: This Keighley Behaviour Support Worker role is based within a mainstream secondary school's on-site provision. It focuses on supporting pupils who need behavioural or emotional support to stay engaged with education. The work includes one-to-one and small-group support, plus time in both the school's specialist provision and mainstream classrooms. The setting is described as structured and supportive, with targeted interventions designed to help pupils re-engage with learning and develop positive behaviours. This is more education-linked than the residential roles above, but it still sits within the wider support-worker brief and is relevant for candidates with behavioural support experience.
summary: Keighley school-based behaviour support role helping pupils with emotional or behavioural needs stay engaged in education.

title: Nights Health Care Assistant
description: This Castleford night-care role is based at Fairburn Vale care home. The source shows both full-time and part-time hours and positions it as a Health Care Assistant post working nights. The available text is truncated, but it is clearly a care-home-based support role rather than community work. It fits the wider care assistant brief for the slice and offers a straightforward permanent night-shift option in West Yorkshire.
summary: Night care assistant role in Castleford supporting residents in a care home on full-time or part-time hours.

title: Care Assistant to Games Development Student
description: Ask Jules is recruiting for a care assistant to support a university student studying Games Development Art at the University of Huddersfield. The role appears to be a personal-assistant-style support post centred on day-to-day assistance for a student while they live and study at university. The advert positions it as a rewarding role with direct personal impact. Based on the available text, this is a more individualised care assignment than the residential roles, but it remains relevant to the broader care assistant category for the slice.
summary: Huddersfield personal care role supporting a university student with daily living alongside their studies.

title: Children's Residential Support Worker
description: This Castleford residential childcare role focuses on the day-to-day support of young people living in children's homes. The source frames it as being present for both difficult and positive moments, helping young people regain stability and move toward adulthood. While the metadata salary fields were blank, the body text suggests earnings may increase with sleep-ins and good attendance. The core of the role is consistent residential support, routines and relationship-based care for children and young people in a home setting.
summary: Children's residential care role in Castleford focused on consistent support, routines and positive outcomes for young people.
